experience.subtitle: Jabal Al Alam
experience.description: Specialized in Building Management Systems (BMS) and low-current GRMS systems, including optimization, commissioning, and troubleshooting of HVAC, DDCs, and MCCs over TCP/IP networks.

experience.description: As the club lead, I oversaw operations and fostered a collaborative environment by coordinating with university departments and mentoring members in technical...

publications.title: Microplastics review in Q1 Elsevier Journal
publications.subtitle: Journal of Hazardous Materials: Plastics
publications.description: As 1st author, I published this paper with my co-authors in the Journal of Hazardous Materials: plastics (Elsevier). The study provides a comprehensive analysis of modern microplastics detection techniques, evaluating their profound ecological impact on marine ecosystems, human health, and environmental sustainability.

publications.title: AlUla Publication in Q2 Springer Journal
publications.subtitle: GeoJournal (Springer Nature)
publications.description: This paper I co-authored in, published in Q2 GeoJournal (Springer Nature), delves into the rich tapestry of AlUla's historical landmarks, cultural heritage, natural resources, and evolving tourism landscape. The study offers insights into sustainable development and heritage preservation.

publications.title: self-healing polymers paper in Q2 Springer Journal
publications.subtitle: Innovative Infrastructure Solutions
publications.description: This research dives into how polymer-based innovations can revolutionize the longevity of concrete through self-healing mechanisms, a crucial step toward smarter, more resilient infrastructure.

projects.title: SPLIT Autonomous Robotic Swarm
projects.subtitle: Snapping Pendulums Literally In Two
projects.description: Designed and fabricated a two-wheeled autonomous robotic swarm architecture optimized for high-density warehouse logistics. This system features a distributed ESP32 network, an MPU6050 sensor fused with a Kalman filter, and a custom cascaded PID control system operating at 200 Hz for flawless inverted pendulum stability. The project is further enhanced by an integrated OpenCV artificial intelligence model, enabling intuitive gesture-based teleoperation and fully autonomous pathfinding.

projects.title: Automated Hospital Supply Sorting PLC
projects.subtitle: Ladder Logic & Machine Learning
projects.description: Designed and simulated an automated medical supply conveyor network using Factory I/O and Siemens TIA Portal. The system utilizes Ladder Logic, sensors, and actuators to route packages to the ER, ICU, and Surgery wings. A companion Python machine learning model (Random Forest) was also developed to predict department demand based on temporal and environmental data.

projects.title: Ionic Plasma Thruster for Marine Propulsion
projects.subtitle: Fuel-less Propulsion System
projects.description: This project focused on designing and implementing a fuel-less ionic propulsion system that generates thrust using high-voltage corona discharge and plasma wind, without any moving parts or fuel. The system utilized arc generator modules connected to ring electrodes to produce directional thrust through ionized air particles.

projects.title: Smart HUB75 LED Panel Display
projects.subtitle: Digital IoT Display System with bluetooth
projects.description: Similar to large Advertisement panels, this project involved designing and implementing an advanced digital display panel system using two chained P3 64x64 HUB75 LED panels, tailored for the Career Center at the University of Prince Mugrin. The system featured real-time content updates, smooth animations, and network connectivity for remote management.

projects.title: Axial-flux Electric Generator
projects.subtitle: DIY Power Generation
projects.description: Partnered with a colleague to design and assemble a low-cost DIY axial flux electric generator. With eight 250-turn coils and 32 magnets mounted on CD discs, we achieved a 3V output, showcasing our resourcefulness and ingenuity. This project earned us second place in the university engineering competition.

projects.title: Wireless Controlled Mini Robotic Arm
projects.subtitle: Industrial IoT Application
projects.description: Worked collaboratively with a team to design and implement a Bluetooth-based robotic arm capable of precise movements for tasks such as picking and placing. This innovative project combined industrial design principles (such as axis movements for robotic arms) and advanced wireless control to create a.

projects.title: Bluetooth-based WS2812 LED Panel
projects.subtitle: ESP32 Wireless Office Panel
projects.description: Designed and built a wireless availability door sign system aimed at improving workplace communication. By integrating the ESP32 microcontroller with a 32x8 WS2812 LED matrix, the system provided real-time status updates via Bluetooth-low-energy (BLE) technology. In this project, we learned a great deal of information about

projects.title: DC Motor Speed Controller using 555 Timer Astable Multivibrator
projects.subtitle: PWM controller
projects.description: In this project, I collaborated with Ibrahim Albarbari and Ghazi Alhartani to design and simulate a high efficiency DC motor speed controller using a 555 Timer IC for an Electronics II course. The core of the system relies on Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) to regulate the average power delivered to the motor by varying the duty cycl

projects.title: Python power monitoring system
projects.subtitle: OOP programming project
projects.description: I supervised the design and implementation of a Python-based power monitoring system software that leverages object-oriented programming (OOP) principles. This innovative software was engineered to efficiently manage and analyze data from electrical devices whi

projects.title: Logic Circuits counter system
projects.subtitle: Shift Registers manipulation
projects.description: Collaborated with a team to develop an Arduino-based counter system that counts using a 74HC165 shift register and presents results in hexadecimal format on a 7-segment display. The project demonstrated our ability to combine hardware and softw

projects.title: MATLAB signals analysis software
projects.subtitle: Applying Signal Processing in MATLAB
projects.description: Collaborated on the creation of a signal analysis software using MATLAB, which simulated noise addition and applied advanced filtering statistical algorithms to clean the signal. The software’s results were displayed graphically for user interpretation, highlighting my technical acume

projects.title: JavaScript To-Do List App
projects.subtitle: Web Development
projects.description: Created an interactive and feature-rich to-do list web application utilizing JavaScript. The app allowed users to manage tasks efficiently by storing data locally in the browser, and it offered functionalities such as due dates, task status tracking, priority levels, and filtering options for enhanced productivity.

projects.title: Rock-Paper-Scissors Game
projects.subtitle: Interactive Web Game
projects.description: This is a Rock-Paper-Scissors game developed using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. The project features an interactive user interface where players can select their choice, while the computer randomly generates its move. The game logic determines the winner, displays results dynamically, and keeps track of the score.

projects.title: Python Interactive COVID-19 Data Visualization Map
projects.subtitle: Data Science & Visualization
projects.description: Designed and developed an online interactive map to visualize COVID-19 statistics locally in Saudi Arabia. Leveraging Python's Folium library and HTML I-frames, the project provided users with insightful visualizations of pandemic trends, regional statistics, and temporal analysis of infection rates.
